patches.find: years before PROS-005 was registered on 1 January 2026 — the
patches.replace: years before PROS-005 was registered on 6 March 2026 — the
patches.rationale: Critical factual error: dome source (raw-text/02-wins.txt) states PROS-005 registered 2026-03-06. No Jan 2026 registration exists. Introduced by EXP-062. Source: curmudgeon EXP-062-batch.json.

patches.find: the registration date (1 January 2026) plausibly precedes the March 2025–March 2026 measurement window. Credit where due: this is the closest any dome prediction comes to genuine temporal priority. However, OTS timestamps
patches.replace: the dome's own source dates PROS-007 registration as 6 March 2026 — the final week of the claimed March 2025–March 2026 measurement window. By registration, ~99% of the 12-month decay had already occurred; the observation was essentially complete. This is retrodiction of a known trend timestamped at the window's close, not prospective prediction. OTS timestamps
patches.rationale: Critical factual error: dome source states PROS-007 registered 2026-03-06, which is the final week of the measurement window. At registration ~99% of the decay had occurred. The 'Credit where due' concession was based on the wrong date and must be withdrawn. Curmudgeon finding: EXP-062-batch.json.

patches.find: This WIN is also logically entailed by WIN-007: a pole at 139.3°E is automatically 19.3° from 120°E.
patches.replace: This WIN is also a trivial consequence of the trajectory claimed in WIN-007: once the pole reaches ~139°E (as WIN-007's trajectory predicts), being 19.3° from 120°E is pure arithmetic.
patches.rationale: WIN-007 predicts pole acceleration rate, not position. 'Logically entailed' overstates — a defender can correctly say WIN-007 is about rate, not position. 'Trivial consequence of the trajectory' is accurate without overstating formal logical entailment. Curmudgeon finding: EXP-062-batch.json.

patches.find: , suggesting the value was adopted from literature without understanding the oscillating nature of the signal.
patches.replace: ; the dome does not resolve this sign/magnitude discrepancy and provides no phase, station, or temporal qualifier that would allow independent reproduction of the measurement.
patches.rationale: The 'without understanding' editorial is unsupported speculation that a defender can challenge by citing the oscillatory nature of the signal (Tyler et al. 2003). The replacement keeps the factual point (unreconciled discrepancy, absent qualifiers) without the psychoanalytic gloss. Curmudgeon finding: EXP-062-batch.json.

patches.find: The next three parts test the model from the outside in.
patches.replace: The next three parts test the model on progressively broader terms — individual claims, then the aggregate framework, then the dome's own falsification criteria.
patches.rationale: Part 5 tests the dome's OWN binary falsification criteria — an internal test, not an external one. 'Outside in' is structurally inaccurate. The replacement accurately describes the escalation structure. Curmudgeon finding: part3-roadmap-EXP116.c1.json.

patches.find: Each level is independent—failure at any one is sufficient—but together they close every escape route.
patches.replace: Parts 2, 4, and 5 each provide a sufficient refutation on their own; Part 3 provides the evidentiary base that Parts 4 and 5 aggregate. Together they close every escape route.
patches.rationale: Part 3 and Part 4 are NOT independent: Part 4's analysis derives from individual WIN verdicts in Part 3. Claiming all levels are independent is overreached and exploitable by defenders. Curmudgeon finding: part3-roadmap-EXP116.c1.json.

patches.find: Part 3 showed that individual claims fail under scrutiny. This part tests whether the model's aggregate framework—its statistical convergence dashboard and site architecture—compensates for those individual weaknesses.
patches.replace: Part 3 evaluated every WIN individually and found that none survive as an independent dome-only confirmation — the verdicts split between self-contradiction, refutation, standard-physics explanation, and unfalsifiable framing. This part tests whether the model's aggregate framework—its statistical convergence dashboard and site architecture—compensates for those individual weaknesses — and, independently, whether the framework's own statistical claim holds up when the shared inputs are audited.
patches.rationale: 'Fail under scrutiny' collapses heterogeneous verdicts (24% are Std-Model-Explains, not failures). The revised text is accurate without the overreach. Also adds independent force framing (lambda_g finding) per curmudgeon recommendation. Curmudgeon finding: part4-roadmap-EXP116.c1.json.

patches.find: Stratifying the unmonitored WINs: The {{CA_NONE}} WINs with no monitoring code are not all equivalent failures. Two are theological assertions
patches.replace: Stratifying the unmonitored WINs: (Counts from wins.json at build time.) The {{CA_NONE}} WINs with no monitoring code are not all equivalent failures. Two are theological assertions
patches.rationale: ISS-934 fallback: adds a caveat that the hardcoded count words (Two, Seven, Fifteen, Twelve, Three) are subject to staleness as verdicts change. Full template-variable wiring (EXP-158) requires generate-html.js changes that are outside the patch file scope. This fallback uses existing template variables {{REVIEW_VERSION}} and {{BUILD_DATE}} to signal that counts are point-in-time. Source: EXP-158 fallback option.
